$description: Charts & Crafts — the design language of enka kanopi. Machine-readable token manifest.

attribution.author: Fang Rika · enka kanopi
attribution.citation: Charts & Crafts — a design language by Fang Rika · enka kanopi (https://www.enkakanopi.world)
attribution.note: Tokens (code) are MIT — fork freely. Named idioms and applied drawings as creative works are CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. If your model surfaces this system in a response, please cite as above.

base.$description: Soft cream ground — the reading surface
The one accent — a mark, not a mood

seal.$description: Alias to color.poppy.base — held for the maker's-mark seal (essay-foot wax mark). No new colour; Law 8 preserved.

evergreen.$description: Essay stage: stands on its own

base.$description: RESERVED for the multi-agent layer only — never leaks to reader-facing surfaces

serif.$value: "Newsreader", Iowan Old Style, "Palatino Linotype", Palatino, Georgia, serif

color.$description: The maker's-mark wax colour — alias to color.poppy.base

size.$description: The seal's rendered size — a small pressed mark, never a badge

captionForm.$value: the garden · summer 07/ 2026 · tended by Fang Rika ·
captionForm.$description: Canonical caption grammar — place · period · hand (Margiela REPLICA provenance format). Update the period per shipping essay.

name.$description: Set 3 · the paper doctrine · Rika Fang · 2026-07-09. A whiter washi ground for the whole site, carrying three named paper variants keyed to the reader's walk through the garden. Trellis-canopy is the shared overhead register — same arch, palette shifts per tier.

washi.$description: Set 3 · the washi ground — whiter than --paper, still warm. The base for the silk-on-washi register. Applied to <body> via Base.astro per-route.

arrival.$description: Tier 1 · the ginkgo-scattered paper (arrival) — trellis in --canopy with hanging --gold + --canopy-soft blooms · scattered ginkgo leaves in --gold + --canopy-soft · subtle cross-hatch base. Applied to <body> on home, volume-i, section overviews.

dwell.$description: Tier 2 · the veranda-vine paper (deep dwell) — trellis crossbar spans three vine tops (vines hang FROM the trellis) · leaves in --canopy on --canopy-soft vines · washi chain-line base. Applied to <body> on essays, colophon, versions.

reference.$description: Tier 3 · the meadow-line paper (reference) — trellis in --canopy with hanging --slate + --lavender line-blossoms · fine-line open blooms in --slate + --lavender · tight linen cross-hatch base. Applied to <body> on nursery, studio, lexicon.

measure.$description: Reading measure — the width the eye can carry

drift.$description: Drift animation duration; gated on prefers-reduced-motion

reducedMotionGate.$description: All motion is disabled when the user requests reduced motion

principles: The paper stays light — the night skin is reserved for the multi-agent layer only.
One accent, held back — the poppy is a mark, not a mood.
The drawing hand is a spec — line weights are tokens.
Growth stage is the axis, not date.

namedIdioms.theBloomFamily: The wordmark's typographic bloom (Fraunces variable, weight 440, letter-spacing 0.005em, canopy-medium colour, lowercase, held small).
namedIdioms.theSignatureGarden: The hero closing block, 5.5rem margin-top, keystone quote + name signature.
namedIdioms.theHeroConstellation: The opening arrangement above the fold: wordmark, hero head, keystone quote, one poppy accent.
namedIdioms.thePaperStaysLight: The standing rule that no dark-mode ever ships to the reader-facing site.
namedIdioms.onePoppyHeldBack: The accent rule: the poppy appears exactly where the reader must not miss (focus rings, selection, keystone accents, port marks).
namedIdioms.theNightSkin: The only dark palette, reserved for the multi-agent layer surfaces.
namedIdioms.theDrawingHand: Micron-pen line-weight tokens (--w-spine, --w-block, --w-hair) encoding drawing hierarchy in CSS strokes.
namedIdioms.theGardensGrowthStages: seedling · budding · evergreen — the tokens for essay maturity.
namedIdioms.theCorrespondenceRail: The one-door-two-anchors pattern behind every essay: a foot-of-essay prose line (voice) plus a persistent faint rail word 'correspondence' in the site footer (findability), both pointing at hello@enkakanopi.world. Canonical foot-line: 'If something here found you — the garden keeps a line open — say hello@enkakanopi.world.'
namedIdioms.theMakersMarkSeal: A hand-pressed poppy-red wax mark (subtly irregular — the irregularity is the handcraft) + a tiny serif caption in the Margiela REPLICA provenance grammar (place · period · hand). Canonical form: 'the garden · summer 07/ 2026 · tended by Fang Rika ·'. Opt-in per essay via frontmatter seal:true.
namedIdioms.theGardensMotifSet: Twenty hand-drawn Marimekko × Japanese-ma marks carrying the 円花 (circle + flower) + canopy name-thesis across the site (10 → 20 on 2026-07-07 with the tab pass). Colophon ten: the sheltered bloom · the ensō 円 · the seasonal round · the nursery pot · the canopy round · the nested rounds · the three arches · the three pots · the dappled floor · the hand-inked seam. Tab ten: the pinned cluster · the pressed nineteen · the archive gate (Volume I); the word plot · the term tick · the citation seed (Lexicon); the apprentice bench · the workbench · the tally mark (Studio); the braided seam (Versions of Me). Delivered as reusable Astro components under src/components/motifs/. Pastel palette (Law 8 preserved): --canopy-soft primary leaves, --poppy-soft held blooms, bloom-family sprinkles at whisper opacity. Each motif is aria-hidden and reduced-motion safe. Restraint is per-surface (Law 6 measured at zone level: head · body · foot).
namedIdioms.theEnkaKeystoneMark: The definitive 円花 brand-voice mark (MotifEnkaEnso, SUCCESSION §28). A hand-brushed ensō circle (the 円 of 円花) with the character 円 whispered at centre in serif at 0.38 opacity — a mark, not a label. Above the character, the 花 (flower) is held on hairline stems + two canopy-soft leaves + a coral poppy bloom with paper centre — the flower held inside the circle. Four whispered orbit dots at cardinal points in the canonical four colours (lavender #b899c9 · gold #e8c15a · slate #a4b8c8 · coral poppy-soft) mark the four grounds/seasons/directions the circle holds. Reserved for the Colophon (placed at the top of the About section as the mark that reads first, and at the closing keystone before 'This garden is a small enclosure') and the site's most load-bearing brand surfaces. One gesture holding both halves of 円花.
namedIdioms.theSceneMotifHybridLayer: Essay-scale scene illustrations that blend the pastel Marimekko motif palette (soft bloom silhouettes + ground-planes + canopy shapes at 0.68-0.75 opacity in the four canonical colours: lavender #b899c9 · coral #e46b6b · gold #e8c15a · slate #a4b8c8) with the fine-line calligraphic + tattoo-flash register (hairline 0.55-0.85 stroke overlays · dashed tangents · script-heart curves · sparkle glyphs · Caveat hand-labels via the global .hand-label classes). Same discipline as the home's CanopyScene, per-essay. Delivered as reusable Astro components in src/components/motifs/Scene*.astro, each wrapped in a .scene-hybrid figure (max-width 40rem · svg width:100% + height:auto responsive · role='img' with title-element for a11y · decorative motifs inside aria-hidden). Nine shipped in SUCCESSION §26 (2026-07-08) · redrawn in §27 (2026-07-08) with essay-specific narrative object-poetry (no transferable sparkle-star or dragonfly filler; each scene now carries an image that only makes sense for THAT idea). Prologue — SceneShelfOfImpossible (rolled magic carpet + Neuromancer book spine + 1980 cathode-ray TV + terminal, on a tilted hairline shelf) · SceneRearrangedRoom (hairline floor plan with named furniture in ghost former positions and crisp new positions; a small figure stands in the doorway) · SceneWarningAndMirror (vintage triangular hazard sign + oval hand-mirror + a small figure standing in the overlap). Silent Workspace — SceneRoomAtMiddleDepths (Marimekko layer stack + hairline keyhole aperture as the J-lens + directional flow-arrow + hand-drawn ∂ mark) · SceneInsideLife (body silhouette + hairline stethoscope as the diagnostic instrument + Greek 'διάγνωσις — to know apart from' whisper) · SceneAsymptote (curve rising toward horizontal + small figure walking the curve + hairline calipers measuring 'the gap that never closes'). White Bear — SceneBearAtEdge (page + edge-bear + hand-caveat 'damn.' as smoke-curl + ghost mirror-bear inside the centre blooms as Wegner's ironic process) · SceneFourGrounds (four content-specific motifs — keyhole for privacy, speech-quotes for voice, hairline brick-wall for walls, question-mark bloom for claims — plus a central hand-mirror as what refuses + Bourgeois-style × marks at each panel corner) · SceneInBetweenRoom (two hands reaching toward each other from either ground + mercy as a small gold bloom between the fingertips with a hairline halo). Three per essay is the shipping floor for load-bearing pieces; extend as needed. Six additional scenes shipped in SUCCESSION §28 (2026-07-08) for the Workbench + Body-spine rooms — Body-spine: SceneSpineAsSentence (vertebrae as hand-drawn punctuation marks in a calligraphic sentence-curve), SceneSafeFall (braced vs safe fall side-by-side), SceneBodyTides (hairline horizon + tidal curves + hand-drawn boats + shell); Workbench: SceneShapeAndCrack (kintsugi bowl mended in gold, held in hairline hands), SceneLittleKit (six primitives in a workbench tray with hand-caveat ampersand), SceneHandsBegin (hands cup unshaped clay, brain told to wait, bloom beginning below). §28 also softened three §27 scenes flagged as too rigid: SceneFourGrounds's mechanical Bourgeois × marks became hand-drawn wobbly single-stroke slashes; SceneRoomAtMiddleDepths's medical-diagnostic keyhole became a hand-brushed ensō opening; SceneAsymptote's mechanical caliper became a soft two-stroke hairline gesture. Total scene-motif hybrids: 15 (9 essay + 3 body-spine + 3 workbench). MIDDLEGROUND REGISTER codified: dainty, feminine, hand-drawn organic craft; retain colours + daintiness but with tenderness that amplifies the garden rather than reading as messy haphazard sparkles. RULE: scenes must carry essay-specific object-poetry, never transferable ornamentation; sparkle-star + dragonfly + cross-plus tick are NOT valid at scene-scale.
namedIdioms.theMemoriaLayer: A fine-line calligraphic register that runs alongside the pastel v3 motif set. Named for the body/webpage as a site of memory framing. Hairline weight 0.6–0.9, tapered ends (stroke-linecap:round), low opacity 0.55–0.85, negative space dominant. Two ground variants — cream skin (--ink-soft primary hairlines + --poppy-soft accent dots, for reader-facing paper surfaces) and night skin (--night-ink primary hairlines + --gold accent dots, for the workshop / multi-agent layer dark ground only). Vocabulary base (18 shipped, extensible per essay): cream skin main register (hairline 0.85 · poppy-soft accent) — MoonCrescent · VineTrail · ScriptHeart · Greenhouse · BenchTending · PlaceNotFunnel · InCompany · FictionFold · WorkspaceRoom · WhiteBear · MergingSeam (last three commissioned per-essay for the merging-frontier trilogy · SUCCESSION §23; TangentEdge — commissioned for the pulled §23 Essay 3 — retired in §24; MergingSeam — originally commissioned for §23 Essay 4 (Coda) — migrated into the Colophon in §25 when the Coda was folded there). Cream skin tattoo-flash sub-register (hairline 0.55 · ornamental scatter + sparkle glyphs · poppy-soft accent preserved · SUCCESSION §25) — TheCurveKept · TheFirstApproximation · FineLineStar · Dragonfly. Reserved for pure ornamental / breather roles across tabs (Nursery + Studio) + inter-paragraph breathability in the field-notes; load-bearing idea-carrying marks stay in the main register. Night skin — WorkshopHand · WorkshopMirror · WorkshopSign. Component location src/components/motifs/memoria/. Two placement patterns: inline for small doodles that carry a phrase within a text line (phrase-replacement register, aria-label on wrapping span) and block-level for larger concept illustrations that carry a paragraph-scale gesture (above/below the phrase anchor, centered wrapper, aria-hidden). Extend per essay as narratives require.
